title: Adapt a comparative policy-implementation design to university AI governance

research_logic: Select contrasting cases that vary on a theoretically important institutional condition.
Reconstruct implementation chronologies from documents and interviews.
Compare within-case process evidence before making cross-case claims.
Evaluate rival explanations for divergent outcomes.
strengths: Chronology prevents outcomes from being interpreted without process evidence.
Rival explanations make the mechanism claim auditable.
context_bound_elements: The original cases have comparable administrative records.
The original policy has a clear implementation start date.
The original outcome measure is unavailable for universities.

user_project.topic: Why universities with similar generative-AI policies develop different classroom implementation practices.
available_materials: Public meeting minutes and guidance documents.
Potential interviews with faculty and administrators.
constraints: Policy implementation dates are partly ambiguous.

required_adaptation_moves.reference_move: Select two cases with strong contrast on one institutional condition.
required_adaptation_moves.required_user_action: Select universities based on governance centralization and policy enforcement while documenting other major differences.

required_adaptation_moves.reference_move: Use a single official implementation date.
required_adaptation_moves.required_user_action: Construct an event chronology with multiple policy and implementation milestones.

required_adaptation_moves.reference_move: Use the same administrative outcome measure across cases.
required_adaptation_moves.required_user_action: Define comparable implementation indicators from documents and interviews, with source-specific uncertainty.

required_claim_boundaries: The comparison may support a bounded explanation of implementation differences.
Transfer to other universities depends on matching institutional conditions.
forbidden_inferences: The selected universities statistically represent higher education.
Temporal sequence alone proves the proposed mechanism.
Missing records can be treated as evidence that an event did not occur.
